questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $\sum_{k=1}^5 \frac{1^3+2^3+\ldots+k^3}{1+3+5+\ldots+(2 k-1)}$ is equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \sum_{k=1}^5 \frac{1^3+2^3+\ldots+k^3}{1+3+5+\ldots .+(2 k-1)} \\ & =\sum_{k=1}^5 \frac{\left(\frac{k(k+1)}{2}\right)^2}{k^2} \\ & \qquad\left[\begin{array}{l}\because n^3=\left(\frac{n(n+1)}{2}\right)^2 \\ \text { and } 1+3+5 \ldots+(2 K-1)=K^2\end{array}\right] \\ & =\sum_{k=1}^5 \frac{(k+1)^2}{4}=\frac{2^2+3^2+4^2+5^2+6^2}{4} \\ & =\frac{4+9+16+25+36}{4}=\frac{90}{4}=22.5\end{aligned}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $a, b$ and $c$ form a geometric progression with common ratio $r$, then the sum of the ordinates of the points of intersection of the line $a x+b y+c=0$ and the curve $x+2 y^2=0$ is
questions.solution:
Since, $a, b$ and $c$ form a geometric progression
$\therefore \quad a=a, b=a r, c=a r^2$
Therefore, given line becomes
$\begin{aligned} & a x+a r y+a r^2 & =0 \\ \Rightarrow & x+r y+r^2 & =0 \\ \Rightarrow & x & =-r y-r^2\end{aligned}$
On putting $x=-r y-r^2$ in given curve $x+2 y^2=0$, we get
$\begin{aligned}-r y-r^2+2 y^2 & =0 \\ \Rightarrow \quad 2 y^2-r y-r^2 & =0\end{aligned}$
$\therefore$ Sum of ordinates $=\frac{r}{2}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $x+y=60, x>0, y>0$, then the maximum value of $x y^3$ is
questions.solution:
Given, $x+y=60$
By $\mathrm{AM} \geq \mathrm{GM}$
$$
\begin{array}{ll} 
& \frac{x+\frac{y}{3}+\frac{y}{3}+\frac{y}{3}}{4} \geq\left(\frac{x y^3}{27}\right)^{1 / 4} \\
\Rightarrow \quad & \frac{x+y}{4} \geq\left(\frac{x y^3}{27}\right)^{\frac{1}{4}} \\
\Rightarrow \quad & \frac{60}{4} \geq\left(\frac{x y^3}{27}\right)^{\frac{1}{4}} \\
\Rightarrow \quad & \frac{x y^3}{27} \leq(15)^4 \\
\Rightarrow \quad & x y^3 \leq 3^3 \times 3^4 \times 5^4 \Rightarrow x y^3 \leq \frac{(45)^4}{3}
\end{array}
$$
$\therefore$ Maximum value of $x y^3$ is $\frac{(45)^4}{3}$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $\theta$ and $\alpha$ lie in $Q_3$. If $\cos (\theta-\alpha), \cos \theta, \cos (\theta+\alpha)$ are in harmonic progression, then $\cos \theta \sec \frac{\alpha}{2}=$
questions.solution:
Given, $\cos (\theta-\alpha), \cos \theta, \cos (\theta+\alpha)$ are in HP
$\begin{array}{lc}\because & \frac{2}{\cos \theta}=\frac{1}{\cos (\theta-\alpha)}+\frac{1}{\cos (\theta+\alpha)} \\ \Rightarrow & \frac{2}{\cos \theta}=\frac{\cos (\theta+\alpha)+\cos (\theta-\alpha)}{\cos (\theta-\alpha) \cos (\theta+\alpha)} \\ \Rightarrow & \frac{2}{\cos \theta}=\frac{2 \cos \theta \cos \alpha}{\cos ^2 \theta-\sin ^2 \alpha} \\ \Rightarrow & \cos ^2 \theta-\sin ^2 \alpha=\cos ^2 \theta \cos \alpha \\ \Rightarrow & \cos ^2 \theta(1-\cos \alpha)=\sin ^2 \alpha \\ \Rightarrow & \cos ^2 \theta=\frac{1-\cos 2 \alpha}{1-\cos ^2 \alpha}=1+\cos \alpha \\ \Rightarrow & \cos ^2 \theta=2 \cos ^2 \frac{\alpha}{2} \Rightarrow \cos \theta \sec \alpha / 2=\sqrt{2}\end{array}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The period of $\tan k y+\sin k y$, where $k=1+4+9+\ldots 20$ terms, is
questions.solution:
$k=1+4+9+\ldots$ upto 20 terms
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \therefore k=\sum n^2=\frac{n(n+1)(2 n+1)}{6}, \text { where } n=20 \\
& \therefore k=\frac{20 \times 21 \times 41}{6}=70 \times 41=2870
\end{aligned}
$$
$\therefore$ Period of tan $k y$ is $\frac{2 \pi}{2870}$
and period of $\sin k y$ is $\frac{2 \pi}{2870}$ i.e. $\frac{\pi}{1435}$
$$
\therefore \text { Required period }=\frac{\pi}{1435}
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If the roots of $x^5-a x^4+b x^3-c x^2+d x-1=0$ are all positive such that their arithmetic mean and geometric mean are equal, then $a+b+c+d=$
questions.solution:
Given equation is $x^5-a x^4+b x-c x+d x-1=0$.
Here, geometrical mean and arithmetic mean are equal then all the roots are equal.
Let $\alpha$ is the root of the equation.
$\frac{\alpha+\alpha+\alpha+\alpha+\alpha}{5}=\left(\alpha^5\right)^{1 / 5}$
Here, sum of all roots is $a$.
$\begin{gathered}
\frac{a}{5}=\alpha \\
a=5 \alpha
\end{gathered}$
$(\alpha \alpha \alpha \alpha \alpha)^{1 / 5}=\frac{-e}{a}=-(-1)=1$
$\begin{aligned}
& \alpha^{5 \times \frac{1}{5}}=1 \\
& \alpha=1
\end{aligned}$
From (i), $a=5$.
Now,
$\begin{aligned}
& \alpha \alpha+\alpha \alpha+\alpha \alpha+\ldots . .+\alpha \alpha(10 \text { times })=b \\
& 10 \alpha^2=b \\
& b=10 \\
& \alpha \alpha \alpha+\alpha \alpha \alpha+\ldots . .+10 \text { times }=c \\
& 10 \alpha^3=c \\
& c=10 \\
& \alpha \alpha \alpha \alpha+\alpha \alpha \alpha \alpha+\ldots \ldots \ldots+5 \text { times }=d \\
& 5 \alpha^4=d \\
& d=5
\end{aligned}$
Therefore, $a+d+c+b=5+5+10+10=30$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.q: Three positive real numbers form an increasing <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>G</mi><mo>.</mo><mi>P</mi><mo>.</mo></math> If the first term is doubled and the second term is increased by three times, the new numbers are in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>.</mo><mi>P</mi><mo>.</mo><mo> </mo></math>Then, find the common ratio of the <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>G</mi><mo>.</mo><mi>P</mi><mo>.</mo></math>
questions.solution:
a,ar,ar2, r>1,a>0

2a,3ar,ar2→AP

⇒6r=2+r2⇒r2-6r+2=0

∴r=3±7, but r>1

⇒r=3+7
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If first three terms of a sequence <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>16</mn></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>,</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>6</mn></mfrac></math> are in geometric series and the last three terms are in harmonic series, then the values of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>b</mi></math> will be
questions.solution:
Given 116,a and b are in GP.

⇒a2=b16  ...i

Also, a,b and 16 are in HP.

⇒b=2×a×16a+16=2a6a+1   ...ii

On solving equations i and ii, we get

a=112,-14

and b=19,1.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: For a real variable $a>1$, consider the points $A_k=\left(k a, a^k\right), k=1,2, \ldots ., n$ in the Cartesian plane. If $\alpha$ and $\beta$ represent respectively the arithmetic mean of $x$-coordinates and the geometric mean of $y$ coordinates of $A_k$, then the locus of the point $P(\alpha, \beta)$ is
questions.solution:
We have,
$\begin{aligned} \alpha & =\frac{a+2 a+3 a+\ldots+n a}{n} \\ & =\frac{a[1+2+3+\ldots+n]}{n} \\ & =\frac{a n(n+1)}{2 n}=\frac{a(n+1)}{2}\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & \text { and } \beta=\left(a \cdot a^2 \cdot a^3 \cdot a^4 \ldots a^n\right)^{1 / n} \\ & \qquad=\left(a^{1+2+3+\ldots n}\right)^{1 / n}=a^{\left(\frac{n(n+1)}{2}\right)^{1 / n}}=a^{\frac{n+1}{2}} \\ & \text { Now, } \beta^2=a^{n+1} \\ & \text { and }\left(\frac{2 \alpha}{n+1}\right)^{n+1}=\left(\frac{2 a(n+1)}{2(n+1)}\right)^{n+1}=a^{n+1} \\ & \therefore \quad \beta^2=\left(\frac{2 \alpha}{n+1}\right)^{n+1}\end{aligned}$
So, locus of point $P(\alpha, \beta)$ is $y^2=\left(\frac{2 x}{n+1}\right)^{n+1}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If the roots of the equation, $8 x^3+6 p x^2+3 q x-27=0$ are in a geometric progression, then $q^2+9 p^2+6 p q+q / p=$
questions.solution:
Let roots of the equation
$8 x^3+6 p x^2+3 q x-27=0 \text { are } \frac{a}{r}, a, \text { ar. }$
$\therefore \quad \frac{a}{r}+a+a r=\frac{-6 p}{8}=\frac{-3 p}{4}$
$\Rightarrow \quad a\left(\frac{1}{r}+1+r\right)=\frac{-3 p}{4}$ $\ldots$ (i)
$\left(\frac{a}{r} \cdot a\right)+(a \cdot a r)+\left(\frac{a}{r} \cdot a r\right)=\frac{3 q}{8}$
$\Rightarrow \quad a^2\left(\frac{1}{r}+r+1\right)=\frac{3 q}{8}$ $\ldots$ (ii)
and $\quad \frac{a}{r} \times a \times a r=\frac{27}{8}$
$\Rightarrow \quad a^3=\frac{27}{8} \Rightarrow a=\frac{3}{2}$ $\ldots$ (iii)
From Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get
$a=\frac{3 q / 8}{-3 p / 4}=-\frac{q}{2 p}$ $\ldots$ (iv)
From Eqs. (iii)and (iv), we have
$\frac{-q}{2 p}=\frac{3}{2} \Rightarrow-q=3 p$
Now, $\quad q^2+9 p^2+6 p q+\frac{q}{p}$
$\Rightarrow \quad 9 p^2+9 p^2+6 p(-3 p)+\frac{(-3 p)}{p}$
$\Rightarrow \quad 18 p^2-18 p^2-3=-3$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $x, y, \mathrm{z}$ are in A.P. and $\tan ^{-1} x, \tan ^{-1} y$ and $\tan ^{-1} z$ are also in A.P., then
questions.solution:
Given, $x, y, \mathrm{z}$ are in A.P.
$\therefore \quad 2 y=x+z... (i)$
Also,
$\begin{aligned}
& \tan ^{-1} x, \tan ^{-1} y, \tan ^{-1} z \text { are in A.P. } \\
\therefore \quad & 2 \tan ^{-1} y=\tan ^{-1} x+\tan ^{-1} z \\
& \Rightarrow \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 y}{1-y^2}\right)=\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{x+z}{1-x z}\right) \\
& \Rightarrow \frac{2 y}{1-y^2}=\frac{x+z}{1-x z} \\
& \Rightarrow \frac{2 y}{1-y^2}=\frac{2 y}{1-x z}... [From (i)] \\
& \Rightarrow 1-y^2=1-x z \\
& \Rightarrow y^2=x z
\end{aligned}$
$\therefore \quad x, y, \mathrm{z} \text { are in G.P. }... (ii)$
From (i) and (ii), 
we get $x=y=\mathrm{z}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The sum of the first <math><mn>10</mn></math> terms of the series <math><mn>9</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>99</mn><mo>+</mo><mn>999</mn><mo>+</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>.</mo></math> is
questions.solution:
S n =( 10−1+100−1+1000−1+.... )
=( 10+100+1000+.... )−( 1+1+1...... )
=10( 10 n −1 10−1 )−n
⇒ S 10 =10( 10 10 −1 9 )−10
=10( 10 10 −1 9 −1 )
=10( 10 10 −1−9 9 )
=10( 10 10 −10 9 )
= 100 9 ( 10 9 −1 )
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi></math> are in <math><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>.</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi></math> . then <math><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi></mrow></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi></mrow></mfrac><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mi mathvariant="normal">c</mi><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">b</mi></mrow></mfrac></math> are in:
questions.solution:
a, b, c in AP

Divide above terms with abc, 1bc, 1ca, 1ab in A.P .

Add  a, b, c respectively

a + 1bc, b+1ca, c+1ab in A.P . (sum of two A.P .'s)
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In a harmonic progression <math><msub><mrow><mi>t</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><msub><mrow><mi>t</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><msub><mrow><mi>t</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msub><mo>,</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>,</mo></math> it is given that <math><msub><mrow><mi>t</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>5</mn></mrow></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>20</mn></math> and <math><msub><mrow><mi>t</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>6</mn></mrow></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>50.</mn></math> If <math><msub><mrow><mi>S</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>n</mi></mrow></msub></math> denotes the sum of first <math><mi>n</mi></math> terms of this, then the value of <math><mi>n</mi></math> for which <math><msub><mrow><mi>S</mi></mrow><mrow><mi>n</mi></mrow></msub></math> is maximum is
questions.solution:
Let the first term be a and the common difference be d, then

 20=1a+4d and 50=1a+5d
On solving, we get,

 d=-3100 and a=17100
Hence, tr=10020-3r>0⇒r<203⇒rmax=6
So, sum is maximum for n=6, as after this terms are negative
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mi></mi><mi>y</mi></math> are positive real numbers and <math><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>4</mn><mi>y</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>5,</mn></math> then the largest possible value of <math><mn>16</mn><msup><mrow><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><msup><mrow><mi>y</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow></msup></math> is
questions.solution:
23x2+34y3=5
3x2+3x2+4y3+4y3+4y3=5
Using A.M≥G.M for 3x2,3x2,4y3,4y3,4y3
3x2+3x2+4y3+4y3+4y35≥3x224y3315
3x+4y5≥32432233x2y315
1≥243x2y315
16x2y3≤3
Maximum value of 16x2y3=3

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Let three positive numbers <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>b</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>c</mi></math> are in geometric progression, such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>b</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>8</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>c</mi></math> are in arithmetic progression and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>b</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>8</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>c</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>64</mn></math> are in geometric progression. If the arithmetic mean of <math><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi>b</mi><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi><mi>c</mi></math> is <math><mi>k</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"></mi></math>, then <math><mfrac><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow><mrow><mn>13</mn></mrow></mfrac><mi>k</mi></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
Here, b2=ac,2b+8=a+c,
b+82=ac+64
⇒b2+64+16b=ac+64a
⇒b+4=4a⇒b=4a-4
2b+8=a+c⇒24a+4=a+c
⇒c=7a+8
b2=ac⇒4a-42=a7a+8
⇒16a2+16-32a=7a2+8a
⇒9a2-40a+16=0
⇒9a-4a-4=0⇒a=49,4
But for a=49⇒b<0
So, a=4⇒b=12 and c=36
⇒k=a+b+c3=523
⇒3k13=313×523=4
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced close="|" open="|" separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></math> are the first three terms of an arithmetic progression, then its sum upto <math><mn>20</mn></math> terms is
questions.solution:
2x+1=x+x-1
(i) Case- Ι :-

x≤-1
2-x-1=x+-x+1⇒-2x-2=1
⇒x=-32
Then the A.P. is →-32,12,52......
S20=202-3+19×2=202×35=350
(ii) Case- ΙΙ :-
-1≤x<1
2x+1=x+-x+1⇒2x+2=1⇒x=-12
Then the A.P. is →-12,12,32......
S20=202-1+19×1=10×18=180
(ii) Case- ΙΙΙ :-
x≥1
2x+1=x+x-1⇒2x+2=2x-1⇒2=-1
(not possible)
Hence, the required sum is 350 or 180
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>7</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mn>9</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>5</mn></math> are subtracted respectively from four numbers in geometric progression, then the resulting numbers are in arithmetic progression. The smallest of the four numbers is
questions.solution:
Let the numbers be a,ar,ar2,ar3 ,
⇒a-2,ar-7,ar2-9 and ar3-5 are in A.P.
⇒a-2+ar2-9=2ar-7 and ar-7+ar3-5=2ar2-9
⇒a+ar2=2ar-3 and ar+ar3=2ar2-6
⇒1+r2=2r-3a and 1+r2=2r-6ar
⇒3a=6ar⇒r=2
⇒3a=2r-1-r2=4-1-4=-1⇒a=-3
So, the numbers are –3,–6,–12,–24
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If the roots of the equation $16 x^3-44 x^2+36 x-9=0$ are in harmonic progression, then its greatest root is
questions.solution:
The given equation is
$16 x^3-44 x^2+36 x-9=0$
Let $\alpha, \beta$ and $\gamma$ are in H.P.
$\Rightarrow \frac{3}{\beta}=\frac{1}{\alpha}+\frac{1}{\gamma}+\frac{1}{\beta} \Rightarrow \frac{3}{\beta}=\frac{\alpha \beta+\beta \gamma+\gamma \alpha}{\alpha \gamma \beta}$
$\Rightarrow \frac{3}{\beta}=\frac{36 / 16}{9 / 16}=4 \Rightarrow \beta=\frac{3}{4}$
Now, $\alpha+\beta+\gamma=\frac{11}{4} \Rightarrow \alpha+\gamma=\frac{11}{4}-\frac{3}{4}=2$  ...(i)
And $\alpha \beta \gamma=\frac{9}{16} \Rightarrow \alpha \gamma=\frac{9}{16} \times \frac{4}{3}=\frac{3}{4}$...(ii)
Solving eqn. (i) and (ii) :
$\alpha=\frac{3}{2}, \gamma=\frac{1}{2}$
$\therefore$ Greatest root is $\alpha=\frac{3}{2}$.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If $a, b$ and $c$ form a geometric progression with common ratio $r$, then the sum of the ordinates of the points of intersection of the line $a x+b y+c=0$ and the curve $x+2 y^2=0$ is
questions.solution:
Since, $a, b$ and $c$ form a geometric progression
$\therefore \quad a=a, b=a r, c=a r^2$
Therefore, given line becomes
$\begin{aligned} & a x+a r y+a r^2 & =0 \\ \Rightarrow & x+r y+r^2 & =0 \\ \Rightarrow & x & =-r y-r^2\end{aligned}$
On putting $x=-r y-r^2$ in given curve $x+2 y^2=0$, we get
$\begin{aligned}-r y-r^2+2 y^2 & =0 \\ \Rightarrow \quad 2 y^2-r y-r^2 & =0\end{aligned}$
$\therefore$ Sum of ordinates $=\frac{r}{2}$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $p, x_1, x_2 \ldots, x_n$ and $q, y_1, y_2, \ldots, y_{\mathrm{n}}$ are two arithmetic progressions with common differences a and $\mathrm{b}$ respectively. If $\alpha$ and $\beta$ are the arithmetic means of $x_1, x_2, \ldots x_n$, and $y_1, y_2, \ldots, y_n$ respectively. Then the locus of $P(\alpha, \beta)$ is
questions.solution:
It is given that $p, x_1, x_2, x_3 \ldots x_n$ and $q, y_1, y_2, y_3 \ldots y_n$ are in A.P. whose common difference are $a$ and $b$ respectively. $$ \begin{aligned} \therefore \quad x_1 & =p+a, x_n=p+n a \\ y_1 & =q+b, y_n=q+n b \end{aligned} $$ Also, given $\alpha$ is A.M. of $x_1, x_2, x_3 \ldots x_n$ $$ \begin{array}{rlrl} \therefore & \alpha & =\frac{x_1+x_2+x_3+\ldots .+x_n}{n} \\ \Rightarrow & \alpha=\frac{n}{2} \frac{\left(x_1+x_n\right)}{n} \\ \Rightarrow & \alpha=\frac{x_1+x_n}{2} \end{array} $$ Similarly, $\beta$ is A.M. of $y_1, y_2, y_3 \ldots y_n$. So, $$ \beta=\frac{y_1+y_n}{2} $$ Thus, substituting the value of $x, x_n, y$ and $y_n$, we get
From Eqs. $(i)$ and (ii) eliminate $(n+1)$, we get $$ \begin{aligned} & \frac{2 \alpha-2 p}{a}=\frac{2 \beta-2 q}{b} \\ \Rightarrow \quad & b(\alpha-p)=a(\beta-q) \end{aligned} $$ Hence, locus of $p(\alpha, \beta)$ is $b(x-p)=a(y-q)$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Let \(P(n): 1^2+2^2+3^2+\ldots+n^2\) \(=\frac{6(n-1)(n-2) \ldots(n-2020)+2 n^3+3 n^2+n}{6}\), for all \(n \in \mathbf{N}\). Then which of the following is correct?
options: \(P(n)\) is not true for any \(n \in N\)
questions.solution:
Given statement
\(\begin{aligned}
& P(n)=1^2+2^2+3^2+\ldots+n^2= \\
& \frac{6(n-1)(n-2) \ldots(n-2020)+2 n^3+3 n^2+n}{6}
\end{aligned}\)
\(\because\) We know that,
\(\begin{aligned}
& 1^2+2^2+3^2+\ldots+n^2=\frac{n(n+1)(2 n+1)}{6} \\
& \therefore \quad \frac{6(n-1)(n-2) \ldots(n-2020)+2 n^3+3 n^2+n}{6} \\
& \quad=(n-1)(n-2) \ldots(n-2020)+\frac{n(n+1)(2 n+1)}{6}
\end{aligned}\)
will be \(\frac{n(n+1)(2 n+1)}{6}\) if \(n=1,2,3, \ldots ., 2020\) only.
Therefore, \(P(n)\) is true for all \(n \leq 2020\).
Hence, option (c) is correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Let \(A, G, H\) and \(S\) respectively denote the arithmetic mean, geometric mean, harmonic mean and the sum of the numbers \(a_1, a_2, a_3, \ldots, a_n\). Then the value of \(x\) at which the function \(f(x)=\sum_{k=1}^n\left(x-a_k\right)^2\) has minimum is
questions.solution:
Given function \(f(x)=\sum_{k=1}^n\left(x-a_k\right)^2\)
\(=\sum_{k=1}^n\left(x^2-2 x a_k+a_k^2\right)\)
\(\begin{aligned}
& =n x^2-2 x\left(a_1+a_2+a_3+\ldots a_n\right) \\
& +\left(a_1^2+a_2^2+\ldots+a_n^2\right)
\end{aligned}\)
\(\because\) The quadratic expression \(a x^2+b x+c\) has its minimum value at \(x=-\frac{b}{2 a}\).
\(\therefore f(x)\) has it's minimum value at
\(\begin{aligned}
& x=-\frac{-2\left(a_1+a_2+a_3+\ldots+a_n\right)}{2 n} \\
& =\frac{a_1+a_2+a_3+\ldots+a_n}{n} \Rightarrow x=A
\end{aligned}\)
Hence, option (4) is correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: $a, b, c, d, e, f, g, h$ be distinct elements in the set $\{-7,-5,-3,-2,2,4,6,13\}$. The minimum value of $(a+b+c+d)^2+(e+f+g+h)^2$ is
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \text {  Let } a+b+c+d=x \text { and } e+f+g+h=y \\
& x^2+y^2=(x+y)^2-2 x y \\
& =(13+6+4+2-2-3-5-7)^2-2 x y \\
& =64-2 x y
\end{aligned}
$$
As we know $\mathrm{AM} \geq \mathrm{GM}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \frac{x+y}{2} \geq \sqrt{x y} \\
\Rightarrow \quad & \frac{8}{2} \geq \sqrt{x y} \\
\Rightarrow \quad & x y \leq 16
\end{aligned}
$$
$x^2+y^2$ is minimum when $x y$ is maximum.
The maximum value of $x y=16$.
The minimum value of $x^2+y^2=64-2 \times 16=32$
$\therefore$ The minimum values of
$$
(a+b+c+d)^2+(e+f+g+h)=32
$$
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Geometric mean of \(\tan 1^{\circ}, \tan 2^{\circ}, \ldots \ldots . . .\), \(\tan 89^{\circ}\) is
questions.solution:
\(\tan 1^{\circ} \cdot \tan 2^{\circ} \ldots \tan 45^{\circ} \cdot \tan 46^{\circ} \ldots \tan 88^{\circ} \cdot \tan 89^{\circ}\) \(\tan 1^{\circ} \cdot \tan 2^{\circ} \ldots \tan 44^{\circ} \cdot \tan 45^{\circ} \tan \left(90-44^{\circ}\right)\) \(\ldots \tan \left(90^{\circ}-1^{\circ}\right)\)
\(\tan 1^{\circ} \cdot \tan 2^{\circ} \ldots \tan 44^{\circ} \tan 45^{\circ} \operatorname{Cot} 44^{\circ} \cdot \operatorname{Cot} 43^{\circ}\)... \(\operatorname{Cot} 1^{\circ}\)
\(\tan 1^{\circ} \cdot \tan 2^{\circ} \ldots \tan 44^{\circ} 1 \frac{1}{\tan 44^{\circ}} \frac{1}{\tan 43^{\circ}} \cdots \frac{1}{\tan 1^{\circ}}=1\)
Hence, option (b) is correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If ab<sup>2</sup>c<sup>3</sup> , a<sup>2</sup>b<sup>3</sup>c<sup>4</sup> , a<sup>3</sup>b<sup>4</sup>c<sup>5</sup> are in AP (a,b,c > o), then the minimum value of a + b + c is
questions.solution:
∵     AM⁡ ≥ GM

⇒     a + b + c 3 ≥ a b c 1 / 3

or      a + b + c ≥3 a b c 1 / 3                  ......(I)

But given

ab2c3 , a2b3c4 , a3b4c5 are in AP (Divide the sequence by ab2c3 )

⇒    1,abc, a2b2c2 are also in AP  ( ∵ a b c ≠ 0 )

⇒    2abc = 1 + a2b2c2

⇒    (abc - 1)2 = 0

∴    abc = 1

Now from Eq. (i),

         a + b + c ≥ 3 1 1 / 3

or      a + b + c ≥ 3

Hence, minimum value of a + b + c is 3.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If AM of two numbers is twice of their GM, then the ratio of greatest number to smallest number is
questions.solution:
Let the numbers be x and y

Since, x+y2=2xy

⇒x+y=4xy    .....(i)

⇒x+y2=16xy

Also x-y2=x+y2-4xy

∴ x-y2=16xy-4xy=12xy

⇒ x-y=23xy ...(ii)

On solving Eqs. (i) and (ii), we get

x=(2+3)xy

and y=(2-3)xy

∴ Required ratio = xy=(2+3)xy(2-3)xy

=2+32=7+43
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>tan</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>9</mn></mfrac></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>tan</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mn>7</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>18</mn></mfrac></mfenced></math> are in arithmetic progression and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>tan</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>9</mn></mfrac></mfenced><mo>,</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>tan</mi><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mn>5</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>18</mn></mfrac></mfenced></math> are also in arithmetic progression, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>|</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mi mathvariant="normal">y</mi><mo>|</mo></math> is equal to :
questions.solution:
x=12tanπ9+tan7π18

and 2y=tanπ9+tan5π18

so, x-2y=12tanπ9+tan7π18

-tanπ9+tan5π18

⇒|x-2y|=cotπ9-tanπ92-tan5π18

=cot2π9-cot2π9=0

astan5π18=cot2π9;tan7π18=cotπ9
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If the sum of the second, third and fourth terms of a positive term G.P. is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>3</mn></math> and the sum of its sixth, seventh and eighth terms is <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>243</mn></math>, then the sum of the first <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>50</mn></math> terms of this G.P. is :
questions.solution:
Let a, ar, ar…2….. be terms of G.P. 

Given,T2+T3+T4=3  ⇒ ar 1+r+r2=3

T6+T7+T8=243  ⇒
ar51+r+r2=243

by (i) and (ii)

r4=81
 ⇒r=3
∴a=113

S50=ar50-1r-1=350-126
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.solution:
By verification method, if \(k=0\)
Then, \(2 \cdot 4^1+3^1=11 t\)
\(\Rightarrow \quad 8+3=11 t \Rightarrow t=1\)
Now, from the second given relation on putting the values of \((k, t)=(0,1)\), we get
\(\begin{aligned}
2 \cdot(64)+81 & =11\left(p+3^q\right) \\
\Rightarrow \quad 128+81 & =11\left(p+3^4\right) \\
\Rightarrow \quad 11\left(p+3^q\right) & =209 \Rightarrow p+3^q=19
\end{aligned}\)
Now, from the option \(p\) must be 16 and \(q=1\)
\(=(3 k+1)_{k=0} .\)
Hence, option (a) is correct.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math><msub><mrow><mi>A</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><msub><mrow><mi>A</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub><mo>;</mo><mi> </mi><mi> </mi><msub><mrow><mi>G</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><msub><mrow><mi>G</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> and <math><msub><mrow><mi>H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub><mo>,</mo><mi> </mi><msub><mrow><mi>H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></math> respectively be two AM's GM's and HM's between two quantities, then the value of <math><mfrac><mrow><msub><mrow><mi>G</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub><msub><mrow><mi>G</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></mrow><mrow><msub><mrow><mi>H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn></mrow></msub><mi> </mi><msub><mrow><mi>H</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msub></mrow></mfrac></math> is
questions.solution:
Let the two quantities be a and b,

then, a, A1, A2, b are in A.P.

∴ A1-a=b-A2   ⇒   A1+A2=a+b ......(i)

Again, a, G1, G2, b are in GP.

∴ G1a=bG2   ⇒   G1G2=ab .......(ii)

Also, a, H1, H2, b are in HP.

∴ 1H1-1a=1b-1H2  ⇒   1H1+1H2=1b+1a

⇒ H1+H2H1H2=a+bab

⇒ H1+H2H1H2=A1+A2G1G2 [using equations (i) and (ii)]

⇒ G1G2H1H2=A1+A2H1+H2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>b</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>c</mi><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>d</mi><mo> </mo><mtext>and</mtext><mo> </mo><mi>p</mi></math> be non-zero distinct real numbers such that <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>b</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>c</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced><msup><mi>p</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><mo>(</mo><mi>a</mi><mi>b</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>b</mi><mi>c</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>c</mi><mi>d</mi><mo>)</mo><mi>p</mi><mo>+</mo><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>b</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>c</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>d</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math>. Then
questions.solution:
Rearranging the expression, we get
(ap-b)2+(bp-c)2+(cp-d)2=0
⇒  ap-b=0,bp-c=0,cp-d=0

⇒  p=ba=cb=dc

Which is condition of GP.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The smallest possible value of p+q+r (where p>6), if 1+3+5+...+p+1+3+5+...+q=1+3+5+...+r where each set of parentheses contains the sum of consecutive odd integers as shown.
questions.solution:
We know that 1 + 3 + 5 + .... + (2k - 1) =k2

Thus, the given equation can be written as p+122+q+122=r+122⇒p+12+q+12=r+12

Therefore, p+1,  q+1,  r+1 forms a Pythagoren triple. As   p>6,  p+1>7 .

The first Pythagorean triple containing a number > 7 is (6, 8, 10).

We may take p + 1 = 8, q + 1 = 6, r + 1 = 10 ⇒p+q+r=21
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: In an arithmetic progression the <math><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>p</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">h</mi></mrow></msup></math> term is twice the <math><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>q</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">h</mi></mrow></msup></math> term. If its <math><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>p</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">h</mi></mrow></msup></math> term is <math><mi>λ</mi></math> times the <math><msup><mrow><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mi>p</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>q</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></mrow><mrow><mi mathvariant="normal">t</mi><mi mathvariant="normal">h</mi></mrow></msup></math> term, then <math><mi>λ</mi></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
For the A.P. let a be the first term and d be the common difference

Given Tp+1=2Tq+1
⇒a+pd=2a+qd⇒a=p-2qd
Also,
T3p+1=λTp+q+1
a+3pd=λa+p+qd
aλ-1=d3p-λp-λq
So, p-2qd=λp+λq-3pd1-λ
⇒p-pλ-2q+2qλ=λp+λq-3p
⇒λp+q+p-2q=p-2q+3p
⇒λ=4p-2q2p-q=22p-q2p-q=2
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q:
Match the following :
 
  Column -I   Column-II
A. If x>1, y>1, z>1 are in G.P., then logex⁡e,  logey⁡e,logez⁡e are in P. A.P.
B. If a,b,c are three consecutive terms of a progression such that a-bb-c=ac then a, b, c are in Q. G.P.
C. If a, b, c are in A.P. then 2ax+1, 2bx+1, 2cx+1 (x≠0) are in R. H.P.
D. If -1<a, b, c<1 & a, b, c are in A.P. & x=(∑n=1∞an)+1, y=(∑n=1∞bn)+1, z=(∑n=1∞cn)+1 then 1x,1y,1z are in S. A.G.P.
    T. None
options: <math><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>→</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>→</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mo>→</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Q</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">D</mi><mo>→</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi></math>

<math><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>→</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>→</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Q</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mo>→</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">D</mi><mo>→</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi></math>

<math><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>→</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>→</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Q</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mo>→</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">R</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">D</mi><mo>→</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi></math>

<math><mi mathvariant="normal">A</mi><mo>→</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Q</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">B</mi><mo>→</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">C</mi><mo>→</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><mi mathvariant="normal"> </mi><mo>;</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">D</mi><mo>→</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">Q</mi></math>
questions.solution:
A. we have

  logex⁡e,  logey⁡e, logez⁡e

⇒11+lnx, 11+lny, 11+lnz

as    x, y, z are in GP

⇒lnx, lny, lnz are in AP

⇒1+lnx,1+ lny,1+ lnz are in AP

⇒11+lnx, 11+lny, 11+lnz are in HP

 

B. a-bb-c=ac

⇒b=2aca+c

⇒a, b, c are in H.P.

 

C. Since a, b, c are in AP

        b-a=c-b=d(say)

⇒2ax+1, 2bx+1, 2cx+1  will be in GP.

as 2bx+12ax+1=2cx+1 2bx+1=2dx constant

D.   Using the formula of the sum of an infinite GP

        x=11-a ⇒1x=1-a
and 
        y=11-b ⇒1y=1-b ,
and
         z=11-c⇒1z=1-c

Since a, b, c are in AP

⇒1-a, 1-b, 1-c are also in AP

Hence 1x,1y,1z are in A.P.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: If <math><mn>11</mn></math> arithmetic means are inserted between <math><mn>28</mn></math> and <math><mn>10</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then the number of integral arithmetic means are
questions.solution:
Let, A1,A2,A3,……,A11 be 11 A.M.’s between 28 and 10
⇒28,A1,A2,……,A11,10 are in A.P

Let, d be the common difference of A.P.
Also, the number of terms =13

⇒10=T13=T1+12d=28+12d
⇒d=10-2812=-1812=-32
⇒ Number of integral A.M.’s are 5
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: <math><mrow><munderover><mo stretchy="false">∑</mo><mrow><mi>r</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>n</mi></mrow></munderover><mrow><mfrac><mrow><mi>r</mi></mrow><mrow><msup><mrow><mi>r</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mrow><mi>r</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>2</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfrac></mrow></mrow></math> is equal to
questions.solution:
Let, Ιr=rr4+r2+1=rr2+r+1r2-r+1
=12r2+r+1-r2-r+1r2+r+1r2-r+1
=-121r2+r+1-1r2-r+1
=-12Vr-Vr-1
⇒∑r=1nΙr=-12Vn-V0
=-121n2+n+1-1
=121-1n2+n+1=12n2+nn2+n+1
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
questions.q: The sum of the <math><msup><mrow><mn>3</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>r</mi><mi>d</mi></mrow></msup></math> and the <math><msup><mrow><mn>4</mn></mrow><mrow><mi>t</mi><mi>h</mi></mrow></msup></math> terms of a <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">G</mi><mo>.</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><mo>.</mo></math> is <math> <mrow> <mn>60</mn> </mrow> </math> and the product of its first three terms is <math> <mrow> <mn>1000.</mn> </mrow> </math> If the first term of this <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">G</mi><mo>.</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">P</mi><mo>.</mo></math> is positive, then its <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mn>7</mn><mrow><mi>t</mi><mi>h</mi></mrow></msup></math> term is:
questions.solution:
Let the 'G.P.' be

ar,a,ar,ar2

Given, 

ar·a·ar=1000

⇒a3=1000

⇒a=10

Also, ar+ar2=60

⇒10r+r2=60

⇒r+r2=6

⇒r2+r-6=0

⇒r-2 r+3=0

So, r=2 or r=-3

As 1st term is positive ∴  r=2

∴ G.P. is '5', '10', '20'.......

Hence, 7th term is 5·26=320.
questions.source: 99 Percentile Qs Bank for JEE Main
